sourceMovie.title: Radio
sourceMovie.whyPeopleLoveIt: Audiences latched onto Radio (2003) as a drama theatrical experience: big enough for a group watch, specific enough to reward a second viewing once you know where it's headed. The film leans hard into its core genre, which is exactly what fans say they wanted more of from blockbusters in that era.

recommendations.title: Forrest Gump
recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Radio clicked for you, Forrest Gump (1994) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with comic timing and sharper banter. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

recommendations.whyYoullLoveIt: If Radio clicked for you, Taxi Driver (1976) is a strong next stop: both trade in drama, with momentum and clean stakes. Expect a different story, but a similar willingness to go big when the moment demands it.

faq.question: What should I watch after Radio?
faq.answer: Try Forrest Gump, Taxi Driver, Donnie Darko—each shares DNA with Radio while changing the setting enough to feel fresh.

faq.question: What genre is Radio?
faq.answer: Radio (2003) is best described as Drama. Stores and algorithms use different labels, but that mix is what fans actually recognize on a rewatch.

faq.question: Is Radio worth revisiting?
faq.answer: Viewer scores sit around 7.3/10 on TMDB—high enough that replays are common for people who like this drama lane.
